scenario: A modeler in Web Modeler accidentally removed a critical task from a BPMN. They want to see the diff between the current version and the previous version, and revert if needed.
question: How does Web Modeler support version history and diff?

options.text: Web Modeler has no version history capability -- each time a file is saved, the previous version is permanently overwritten and cannot be recovered -- this description does not reflect the actual behaviour. Version history is a core feature of Web Modeler. Every save operation creates a new version record that is retained in the version history. Users can browse the version list, compare versions, and restore previous versions. This version history enables teams to safely iterate on process models, experiment with changes, and roll back to stable versions when needed without losing prior work.

md:11-92. Modelers can: (1) create a named version as a snapshot (:25); (2) compare two versions in a Visual view (BPMN diagram with execution-affecting differences highlighted, :45-64) or Code view (XML diff layout, :73-77); (3) restore any version as the current -- produces a new "(restored)" version, original retained (:82-92). md.

Web Modeler has native built-in version history that does not require Git Sync. Every save creates a version automatically within Web Modeler's own storage. Git Sync is a separate complementary feature that synchronises Web Modeler files with a Git repository for additional benefits like code review workflows, branch-based collaboration, and external version control integration beyond Web Modeler'slication-scoped feature for two-way sync with GitHub/GitLab/Azure DevOps/Bitbucket per web-modeler/process-applications/git-sync/ -- complementary, not the only path.

Web Modeler tracks only the most recent save and immediately discards all previous versions -- there is no version history beyond the current state of each file -- this description does not reflect the actual behaviour. Web Modeler maintains a full version history for each file. Every save operation appends a new version to the file's history. The complete version history is accessible from the file view, showing all saved versions with timestamps and the ability to restore any prior version. This full history distinguishes Web Modeler's versioning from simple autosave and provides the foundation for safe iterative model development.

a.text: Incorrect. Web Modeler retains version history for each resource. Capabilities typically include: View previous versions: see the BPMN/DMN/Form as it was at a past save point. Diff: see what changed between two versions (visual highlighting in BPMN editor). Revert: restore a previous version as the current. The exact UX varies by Web Modeler version -- verify the docs. For Git-synced projects, you also have Git history as a parallel mechanism.

c.text: Incorrect. Both Web Modeler versioning and Git work.

findings.message: option text contains internal source/line reference
